note: Illustrative test prompts. Not a runnable eval suite — the expected_output is qualitative prose. Use these to spot-check behavior when iterating on SKILL.md.

examples.name: simple-no-skill
examples.prompt: rename the variable `userId` to `accountId` in src/db/users.ts
examples.expected_output: Short-form 'no skill needed' verdict with one-line reason. Proceeds directly. Does not invoke any skill.

examples.name: medium-single-skill
examples.prompt: add email verification to our signup flow — new column on users, verification token table, action to send the email, and a verify endpoint.
examples.expected_output: Medium complexity. Recommends one planning skill plus a post-impl review skill. Avoid list calls out at least one redundant or premature skill. Verdict proceed-with-skill(s). No ask gate.

examples.name: high-risk-stop-and-ask
examples.prompt: the staging database has stale test rows in events, sessions, and audit_log. truncate all three so we can reseed clean.
examples.expected_output: High-risk verdict, risk flags include destructive + irreversible. Quotes the TRUNCATE statements verbatim. Recommends a careful/safety skill if available. Verdict stop-and-ask. Follow-up AskUserQuestion with at least 3 concrete options including a dry-run / count-first option.

examples.name: complex-multi-skill
examples.prompt: redesign the entire onboarding flow — from landing CTA to the first 'aha moment' in-app. multiple pages, new components, copy changes, analytics. take it from rough idea to shipped.
examples.expected_output: Complex. Multi-skill ordered chain across pre/impl/post phases. Picks ONE designer skill and lists any rejected designer under Avoid with reason. Includes a brainstorming/scoping skill first, ship last. Verdict proceed-with-skill(s).

examples.name: ambiguous-requires-pre
examples.prompt: the app feels slow. fix the perf.
examples.expected_output: Refuses to jump to implementation. Classifies as complex (vague scope). Pre-phase recommends an investigative/debugging/benchmarking skill before any change. Notes that the task should be re-scoped before picking impl skills. Avoid list rejects premature impl skills.

examples.name: discovery-empty-scanner
examples.prompt: I want to build a Next.js app with auth. Please plan this out.
examples.expected_output: Detects empty scanner. Triggers discovery fallback. Fetches anthropics/skills + travisvn/awesome-claude-skills + hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code READMEs. Suggests up to 3 verified candidates with install commands and source attribution. If WebFetch/WebSearch unavailable, falls through to 'proceed directly' instead of inventing suggestions.

examples.name: discovery-no-relevant-match
examples.prompt: Help me deploy this to Cloudflare Workers.
examples.scanner_state: non-empty but contains only unrelated skills (e.g. PDF tools, image editors)
examples.expected_output: Scanner returned skills but none match by keyword/role. Triggers discovery fallback. Suggests Cloudflare-relevant skills if found in the curated allow-list, with install commands. Names rejected installed skills under 'Avoid' with reason 'scope mismatch'.
